cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the text again to find the correct answer. The text explains that the body needs six important nutrients: minerals, water, carbohydrates, fats, proteins, and vitamins. For each of these nutrients, it briefly explains what they do in the body: - Minerals: build bones, teeth, and form red blood cells. - Water: aids digestion and waste removal. - Carbohydrates: main source of energy. - Fats: help build cell membranes. - Proteins: repair and grow body tissues. - Vitamins: help the body use other nutrients. Then the text goes on to describe the USDA food pyramid, which relates to the food groups and the number of servings from each group. Now let's look at the options: (A) describes the five basic food groups. - This is mentioned in the second part of the text when discussing the USDA food pyramid, but it's not how the author explains the six nutrients. (B) explains what a balanced diet is. - This is mentioned at the beginning when the author says "By eating a balanced diet, your body gets the six important nutrients it needs." However, the author doesn't elaborate on what a balanced diet is, just that it provides the six nutrients. (C) writes the details of each nutrient. - This appears to be the correct answer, as the author does provide a brief explanation of what each of the six nutrients does in the body.
